LHX.sentence: L3Harris has slipped about 5% and is now well off its highs, which is unusual for a steady defense contractor like this — but the underlying business of military communications and electronic warfare gear hasn't changed.

ASML.sentence: ASML keeps marching higher and has run up quite a bit lately, which is normal for the only company in the world that makes the machines needed to print the most advanced chips.

LIN.sentence: Linde is having a quiet, flat day — they supply oxygen and other industrial gases under long contracts and nudge prices up steadily, which is the whole story.

MSFT.sentence: No fresh data on Microsoft today, so there's simply nothing to report.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher had no data come through today, so there's nothing new to flag on the lab-equipment giant.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is flat and pays a nice income from its oil-and-gas pipelines, but that payout isn't much better than what you'd earn risk-free right now, so the cushion is thin.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow had no data today — nothing to report on the company that helps big organizations automate their IT and HR busywork.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore dropped about 4% even as copper prices have been surging, which is a bit odd since they mine a lot of copper and tend to catch up to the metal eventually.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin has fallen well below its recent average and is in a deep slump — painful right now, but that kind of washout is often where bargains appear.

KOF.sentence: The big Latin American Coca-Cola bottler ticked up a bit and looks reasonably priced for such a steady, everyday business, with the Mexican currency holding firm.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras slipped a touch, and while oil prices are still healthy, its earnings look like they may be near a peak — meaning the good times may be closer to the top than the bottom.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk eased about 2.5%, and its ships are now valued at less than the cost of replacing the fleet itself, which can happen when global shipping demand is soft.
